query_normalized: quantifying the impact of data resolution on simulated fluid turbulence physics
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) by quantitatively investigating the convergence (a proxy for resolution dependence) of a simulation method to the physical limit in a flow regime that includes "unsteady fluctuations" and "moderately turbulent flows," directly addressing the mechanism of how simulation fidelity relates to physical turbulence.
